title: Area of Triangles
contentObjective: I can find the area of a triangle using the formula A = ½ × base × height.
languageObjective: I can explain my steps using the words base, height, area, and perpendicular.

turnAndTalk.question: At the build site you survey a triangular garden bed with a base of 12 feet and a height of 8 feet. Which measurement is the height, and how is it different from the slanted side?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The height is the perpendicular distance from the base to the top corner.

stems.en: The height is the ___ distance from the base to the top corner.
stems.es: La altura es la distancia ___ desde la base hasta la esquina de arriba.

stems.en: It is different from the slanted side because ___.
stems.es: Es diferente del lado inclinado porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students identify the height as the perpendicular (straight-up, 90-degree) distance from the base to the opposite vertex, not the slanted side.
turnAndTalk.extend: Does it matter which side of the triangular panel you call the base? Justify your thinking using the formula A = 1/2 x b x h.
extendStems: It ___ matter which side is the base because ___.
No matter which base I choose, the area is the same since ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Your triangular panel (base 12, height 8) fits inside a 12 by 8 rectangle. Why do we divide by 2 to find the triangle's area before cutting the glass?
turnAndTalk.kernel: A triangle is exactly half of a rectangle with the same base and height.

stems.en: A triangle is half of a ___ with the same base and height.
stems.es: Un triángulo es la mitad de un ___ con la misma base y altura.

stems.en: So the area is 1/2 times 12 times 8, which equals ___.
stems.es: Entonces el área es 1/2 por 12 por 8, que es ___.

stems.en: The rectangle's area is ___, so the triangle is ___.
stems.es: El área del rectángulo es ___, entonces el triángulo es ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: A strong answer states the triangle is half of the 12 x 8 = 96 rectangle, so the panel area is 48 sq ft, and connects the 1/2 to that halving.
turnAndTalk.extend: Two glass panels both have area 48 sq ft. Must they have the same base and height? Justify or give a counterexample.
extendStems: They ___ have the same base and height because ___.
A counterexample is a triangle with base ___ and height ___, which also gives ___.

turnAndTalk.question: At the client review, the homeowner paints a triangular A-frame wall with base 20 ft and height 14 ft; one can covers 50 sq ft. Talk through how many cans the budget needs.
turnAndTalk.kernel: Find the triangle area first, then divide by what one can covers.

stems.en: The wall's area is 1/2 times 20 times 14, which equals ___ square feet.
stems.es: El área de la pared es 1/2 por 20 por 14, que es ___ pies cuadrados.

stems.en: She needs ___ cans because ___ divided by 50 equals ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compute area = 140 sq ft, then 140 / 50 = 2.8, and reason she must round up to 3 cans to cover the whole wall.
turnAndTalk.extend: Why can't the budget order exactly 2.8 cans? Explain what rounding up means in this real situation and how it changes the leftover paint.
extendStems: She must round up to ___ cans because ___.
The leftover paint covers about ___ sq ft, since ___.

mission.title: The Triangular Skylight
mission.story: You are the lead architect at Sky Harbor Studio. The city has hired your firm to design a glass skylight made of triangular panels for the new transit hub. Before the glass can be cut, you must master the area of triangles — every wrong measurement wastes expensive glass.
mission.objective: Calculate the area of triangular panels accurately so the skylight fits perfectly.

scenes.name: Site Walkthrough
scenes.text: Survey the build site and notice the triangular garden beds the client wants measured.

scenes.name: Drafting Table
scenes.text: Learn the language of the blueprint: base, height, area, and the triangle formula.

scenes.name: Model Workshop
scenes.text: Fold a flat net into a 3D solid to see how 2D faces build real structures.

scenes.name: Panel Calculations
scenes.text: Work the panel orders. The studio adapts the difficulty to keep you in the zone.

scenes.name: Client Review
scenes.text: Apply triangle area to a real paint-and-glass budget for the client.

scenes.name: Project Debrief
scenes.text: Sign off on the build and show the client what you learned.

simulation.instructions: Fold this flat net into a 3D solid. Watch how the six 2D faces wrap into a closed box — every face is a shape whose area you can compute.

question.stem: When this net folds up, what solid does it form?

finale.title: Skylight Approved!
finale.text: The city signed off on your triangular skylight design. Sky Harbor Studio just landed its biggest contract — thanks to your triangle area mastery.

vocabulary.definition: A side of the triangle you use to find the area.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un lado del triángulo que usas para hallar el área.
vocabulary.visual: If the bottom of a triangle is 10 cm, then b = 10 cm in the formula A = ½ × b × h
vocabulary.cloze: The side of a triangle used as the bottom for measuring area is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: The straight-up distance from the base to the top corner.
vocabulary.definitionEs: La distancia recta desde la base hasta la esquina de arriba.
vocabulary.visual: A dashed vertical line from the top point straight down to the base at a 90° angle
vocabulary.cloze: The perpendicular distance from the base to the opposite vertex is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: How much space is inside a flat shape.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Cuánto espacio hay dentro de una figura plana.
vocabulary.visual: A triangle with b = 8 and h = 6 has area = ½ × 8 × 6 = 24 sq units
vocabulary.cloze: The amount of flat space inside a triangle is its ___.

vocabulary.definition: Two lines that meet to make a square corner (90 degrees).
vocabulary.definitionEs: Dos líneas que se unen formando una esquina recta (90 grados).
vocabulary.visual: The corner of a book or a door frame meets at exactly 90°
vocabulary.cloze: Two lines that meet at a 90° angle are ___.

examples.text: Two lines meeting at a 90° angle
examples.why: Perpendicular lines form a right angle.
Those are parallel lines, not perpendicular.

vocabulary.definition: A shape made by putting two or more simple shapes together.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una figura formada al juntar dos o más figuras simples.
vocabulary.visual: A house shape = a rectangle (walls) + a triangle (roof)
vocabulary.cloze: A shape made of two or more basic shapes combined is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: A math rule written with symbols.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una regla matemática escrita con símbolos.
vocabulary.visual: A = ½ × b × h means Area equals one-half times base times height
vocabulary.cloze: A math rule written with symbols, like A = ½ × b × h, is a ___.

launch.narrative: Your architecture firm is designing a park with triangular garden beds. The client wants to know exactly how much soil to order, which means calculating the area of each triangular section. The first garden has a base of 12 feet and a height of 8 feet.
launch.contextImage: Architectural blueprint with triangular garden sections marked in green
noticePrompts: What shape are the garden beds?
What measurements do we need to find the area?
How is the area of a triangle related to the area of a rectangle?
wonderPrompts: Why do we divide by 2 when finding the area of a triangle?
Does it matter which side we call the base?

explore.instructions: Plot the vertices of a triangle with base 12 and height 8.
explore.label: Plot the three vertices of the triangular garden bed.

discourse.prompt: The triangle fits inside a 12 × 8 rectangle. How does the triangle's area compare to the rectangle's area?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The area of the triangle is ___ the rectangle because A = 1/2 × ___ × ___ = ___ square feet.

approaching.stem: What is the area of a triangle with base 10 cm and height 6 cm?
approaching.scaffold: Use A = ½ × b × h. First multiply base × height, then take half.
approaching.explanation: A = ½ × b × h = ½ × 10 × 6 = 30 square centimeters.

approaching.stem: A triangle has an area of 24 sq ft and a base of 8 ft. What is the height?
What is the area of a triangle with base 14 ft and height 6 ft?
A triangular window pane has a base of 9 in and a height of 4 in. What is its area?
approaching.scaffold: Use A = ½ × b × h. Multiply 9 × 4 first, then take half.

approaching.stem: Which formula gives the area of a triangle with base b and height h?
approaching.scaffold: A triangle is half of a rectangle with the same base and height. Which formula shows 'half'?
approaching.explanation: A triangle covers exactly half of a rectangle with the same base and height, so A = ½ × b × h.

onLevel.label: Match each shape to its area.

onLevel.title: Find Dana's Mistake

workedExample.label: Problem
workedExample.work: Find the area of a triangle with base 10 ft and height 7 ft.

onLevel.correctWork: Dana forgot the ½. The area is half of base × height: A = ½ × 10 × 7 = ½ × 70 = 35 square feet.
hints: Look at the multiply step — did Dana use every part of the formula A = ½ × b × h?
The ½ is missing. A triangle is only half of the rectangle, so you must take half of 70.

onLevel.stem: A triangular sail has an area of 30 sq m and a height of 12 m. What is its base?

extending.prompt: An architect designs a triangular roof gable with a base of 18 ft and a height of 9 ft. A second gable has the same area but a base of 27 ft. Find the area of the first gable, then determine the height of the second gable. Show all your work.
extending.sentenceFrame: The first gable's area is ___ sq ft because A = ½ × ___ × ___. The second gable has the same area, so ___ = ½ × 27 × h, which means its height is ___ ft.

extending.stem: A composite logo is made of a rectangle (8 cm by 6 cm) with a triangle on top. The triangle has a base of 8 cm and a height of 5 cm. What is the total area of the logo?
extending.explanation: Rectangle: 8 × 6 = 48 sq cm. Triangle: ½ × 8 × 5 = 20 sq cm. Total: 48 + 20 = 68 sq cm.

extending.prompt: A rectangle is 12 ft by 8 ft. A diagonal line cuts it into two triangles. What is the area of each triangle? Explain how the triangle area formula relates to the rectangle area formula.
extending.sentenceFrame: The rectangle's area is ___ because ___. Each triangle's area is ___ because a diagonal splits a rectangle into ___ equal triangles.

optional.stem: What is the area of a triangle with base 16 m and height 5 m?
A triangle has an area of 36 sq cm and a height of 9 cm. What is its base?
Two triangles have the same area. One has a base of 12 ft and a height of 6 ft. The other has a base of 9 ft. What is the height of the second triangle?
optional.explanation: First area = ½ × 12 × 6 = 36 sq ft. Then 36 = ½ × 9 × h → 36 = 4.5h → h = 8 ft.

connect.scenario: A homeowner wants to paint one triangular wall of an A-frame cabin. The wall has a base of 20 feet and a height of 14 feet. One can of paint covers 50 square feet.
connect.promptQuestion: How many cans of paint does the homeowner need?
connect.prompt: The wall's area is ___ sq ft because A = 1/2 × ___ × ___. She needs ___ cans because ___ ÷ 50 = ___.

exitTicket.stem: A triangle has a base of 11 inches and a height of 8 inches. What is its area?
